Primary forward value: creating quality shots, chances, xG, and dangerous passes.
Connects the individual to the AUS team-success model through Corsi, Fenwick, and xG differential.
Rewards entries, attack creation off the rush, and possession gains into the offensive zone.
Controls turnovers, pass quality, and risk in NZ/DZ/OZ situations.
Captures suppression, takeaways, blocking, and defensive involvement without over-rewarding players stuck defending.
Rewards winning pucks and extending possession, while using attempts + wins to avoid small-sample percentage traps.
Used when PP/PK data is populated; dynamically redistributed when missing.
Penalties, errors, and high-risk losses should reduce score.
Most important defense value: suppress xG, block dangerous shots, retrieve pucks, and reduce quality against.
Core defense skill: manage DZ/NZ pucks, make first passes, and exit cleanly.
Corsi, Fenwick, net xG, and territory indicate whether play moves the right way with player on ice.
Rewards breakout support, passing entries, and controlled transition assistance.
Defenders must win/recover pucks under pressure, especially in DZ.
Rewards secondary offensive contribution without scoring defense like forwards.
PK/PP details if available; dynamic redistribution when missing.
Defensive errors and penalties can be high-cost.
Core goalie output: stop pucks relative to shots faced and goals allowed.
Best chance-quality adjustment: did goalie outperform or underperform expected goals against?
Important because team success depends on surviving dangerous chances.
Prevents the model from overvaluing tiny samples or easy workloads.
Supports exits and reduces pressure through passing decisions.
Low-weight context for penalties and shootouts.
SKATER METRIC WEIGHTS — FORWARDS AND DEFENSE
Scoring chances - total; Time on ice
Directly tied to AUS offensive success.
Shots on goal; Time on ice
Inner slot shots - total; Time on ice
Passes to the slot; Time on ice
Passes to the slot, pre-shot passes
Measures setup play leading to shots.
Website calculates from CORSI+ and CORSI- with fallback to provided %.
Best territorial proxy from team model.
Fenwick for; Fenwick against; Fenwick for, %
Website calculates from for/against with fallback to provided %.
Net xG (xG player on - opp. team's xG); Time on ice
Team xG when on ice; Time on ice
Shows offensive environment when player is on.
Opponent's xG when on ice; Time on ice
Low weight for forwards; still important.
Entries via pass; Time on ice
Entries via stickhandling; Time on ice
Entries via dump in; Time on ice
Breakouts via pass; Time on ice
Puck losses in NZ; Time on ice
Puck losses in DZ; Time on ice
Puck control time; Time on ice
Error leading to goal; Time on ice
Opponent's xG when on ice; Time on ice
Takeaways in OZ; Time on ice
Blocked shots from the slot; Time on ice
Use as context; high value is not automatically good.
Display only unless combined with suppression.
EV DZ retrievals; Time on ice; Opponent xG; Puck losses DZ
Reward retrievals only when paired with low DZ losses and low opp xG.
Puck battles won; Time on ice
Shots on goal; Time on ice
xG conceded; Goals against; Time on ice
Use provided value; higher means goals came from higher-quality chances

TEAM SUCCESS DRIVER TO PLAYER/GOALIE KPI MAPPING
xg_per60; xg_per_shot; goals_per60 as low-weight outcome check
Strongest predictor of winning in AUS. Measures how efficiently the team converts expected-goal chances into goals.
Even-strength shot-attempt share. Best proxy for territorial control and sustained offensive pressure at 5-on-5.
High-danger shot volume. Directly linked to goal probability. Tier 1 offensive process driver.
On-net shot volume. Consistent Tier 1 predictor of offensive activity and pressure generation.
N/A direct; goalie contributes through gsae_per60
xG differential. Best combined on-ice metric — accounts for both offensive creation and defensive concession.
Total shot attempts. Strong proxy for offensive puck possession and sustained territorial pressure.
Quality and volume of scoring chances allowed. Primary defensive process measure. Lower = better.
Cumulative expected goals generated. Volume of quality chance creation — closely paired with xG conversion.
pp_shots_per60; pp_retrievals context; goals_per60 low weight
Power play goals scored. Most direct measure of man-advantage execution.
OZ possession sequences that produce a shot. Highest-quality measure of productive offensive zone time.
special teams module only if PP attempts/TOI available
PP conversion rate. Efficiency of man-advantage situations. Strong special teams predictor.
Total zone entries. Drives offensive possession — more entries means more shots and more OZ time.
Passes delivered into the high-danger slot area. Key pre-shot movement indicator. Dal priority development area.
No direct individual mapping; store as team context only.
Total offensive zone possession time. Broad measure of sustained forward pressure.
Offensive zone possession sequences. Correlated with controlled zone time and shot generation.
Even-strength puck retrievals in own zone. Higher count signals prolonged defensive zone time. Lower = better.
Even-strength puck retrievals in offensive zone. Reflects ability to sustain pressure and win puck battles up ice.
missed_shots context; do not weight high
Off-target shot volume. Reflects overall shooting activity and offensive zone presence.
PK success rate. Strong predictor of special teams defensive performance.
Neutral zone turnovers. Interrupts transition and generates direct counter-attacks. Dal priority defensive gap.
Passes completed immediately before a shot. Measures pre-shot movement quality — reflects shot setup.
No direct individual mapping; store as team context only.
Total defensive zone possession time. Lower = less time under pressure in own end.
Pass completion rate. Reflects puck management quality and execution under pressure.
No direct individual mapping; store as team context only.
Shot accuracy rate (shots on goal ÷ total shots). Reflects shot quality and net presence.
Turnovers in own defensive zone. Creates immediate high-danger scoring opportunities for opponents.
Puck battle events in own zone. Fewer DZ battles = less time under defensive pressure.
Rate of puck battle success across all zones. Reflects compete level at board battles.
Offensive zone possession time. Broader territorial control measure — overlaps with OZ play.
No direct individual mapping; store as team context only.
Steals by the team in own defensive zone. High count signals prolonged DZ pressure.
Defensive zone possession time. Lower = less time under pressure in own end.
No direct individual mapping; store as team context only.
xG required per goal scored. Lower = more clinical finishing relative to shot quality generated.
Zone exit events from own end. Fewer breakouts needed signals less time defending own zone.
No direct individual mapping; store as team context only.
xG opponent requires per goal scored against Dal. Higher = opponents shooting inefficiently.
No direct individual mapping; store as team context only.
Outcome metric. Scoring is the objective of offensive play, but kept low-weighted so process indicators — xG, scoring chances, OZ possession — remain dominant.
No direct individual mapping; store as team context only.
Average xG value of each opponent shot allowed. Lower = better shot suppression and defensive structure.
Average shot quality generated. Higher = better shot selection and chance quality.
No direct individual mapping; store as team context only.
Neutral zone puck battles. Lower count may reflect cleaner transition play.
Convert Time on ice strings like 369:01 into minutes.
minutes = hours_or_minutes_component + seconds/60 depending export; validate max value by season context.
Use for all count stats affected by ice time.
Do not calculate if TOI is null or 0.
Use F vs F, D vs D, G vs G.
Examples: DZ losses, NZ losses, penalty time, goals against.
Use raw attempts + successes; shrink tiny samples toward baseline.
Use for puck battles, pass accuracy, faceoffs, goalie saves.
Prevent 4/4 beating 15/20 just because of raw %.
Score adjusted % and won battles /60 together.
Reward accuracy but protect against low pass samples.
Use raw accurate passes and passes.
Use for/against event counts when available.
Fallback to provided % if raw parts missing.
Use 0.900 baseline if no league average; sample_weight 100 shots.
Use baseline from team/league if available; sample_weight 25 chances.
Goals/assists/points/plus-minus are context or low weight.
Use low final weight or display-only flags.
Do not let outcomes dominate process model.
High DZ retrievals/battles can mean good work or too much DZ time.
Use composites with opp xG, puck losses, and breakouts.
Do not score raw DZ volume as purely positive.
Dashes/blanks should become null, not zero.
If denominator missing, set derived metric null.
Redistribute weights across active metrics in same category.
If a metric/category is unavailable, redistribute within active metrics.
category_score = weighted average of available metric scores only
Do not punish a player for untracked PP/PK columns.
player_id uuid PK; instat_player_name text; jersey_number int; position text; shoots text optional; active boolean
Roster identity. Do not put stats here.
goalie_id uuid PK; instat_player_name text; jersey_number int; active boolean
Can also use players table with position='G', but separate table is simpler for goalie model.
id uuid PK; game_id FK; player_id FK; uploaded_file_id FK; raw jsonb; toi_seconds int; games_played int optional; position text; all extracted stat columns numeric nullable
Use for game-by-game if available. If only season aggregate, create player_season_stats too.
id uuid PK; season text; player_id FK; uploaded_file_id FK; raw jsonb; toi_seconds int; all extracted stat columns numeric nullable
id uuid PK; season text; goalie_id FK; uploaded_file_id FK; raw jsonb; toi_seconds int; all goalie extracted stat columns numeric nullable

metric_weight_id uuid PK; model_type text; category text; category_weight_pct numeric; metric_key text; source_columns text[]; direction text; normalization text; score_method text; metric_weight_in_category_pct numeric; final_weight_pct numeric; include_in_score boolean; sample_rule text
same shape as player_metric_weights but model_type='Goalie'
Separate table or shared metric_weights table with model_type.
id uuid PK; player_id FK; season/game_id; metric_key text; raw_value numeric; calculated_value numeric; score_0_100 numeric; reliability_flag text
id uuid PK; player_id FK; season/game_id; model_type text; overall_score numeric; category_scores jsonb; reliability_score numeric; context_flags jsonb; calculated_at timestamptz
id uuid PK; goalie_id FK; season/game_id; overall_score numeric; category_scores jsonb; reliability_score numeric; context_flags jsonb; calculated_at timestamptz
rank int; team_stat text; team_codex_key text; player_metric_keys text[]; goalie_metric_keys text[]; mapping_type text; note text
Keeps player model tied to AUS success model.
existing table; add file_type text if needed: team_stats, skater_stats, goalie_stats, metric_weights
Link every import to file source.
Import should fail loudly if required columns missing: Player, Position, Time on ice, Games played.
Convert '-' and blank strings to null. Do not convert dash to zero unless the raw metric truly means zero.
Convert '40%' to 0.40 internally. Keep original raw value in raw jsonb for audit.
Convert Time on ice to seconds/minutes. Validate that season aggregate TOI values are plausible.
Skater model uses Position F/D. Goalies use goalie file, not skater model.
Do not calculate per-60 if TOI is null or zero.
Require attempts denominator. If denominator missing, use provided % only as display/QA, not score.
Full reliability 15+ games or meaningful TOI. Moderate 8-14. Low 4-7. Very low fewer than 4.
Within each model/category, active metric weights should renormalize to 100 among available metrics.
Every metric score, category score, and overall score must be clamped between 0 and 100.
Do not use height, weight, nationality, date of birth, or contract fields in performance scoring.
Dashboard should show what drove each score, not just final ranking.
Add player/goalie season stat tables, metric weight tables, calculated metrics, scores, and mapping tables.
Handle skater export, goalie export, and team weight model. Preserve raw jsonb and normalize numeric columns.
Import this workbook’s Skater_Metric_Weights and Goalie_Metric_Weights into Supabase.
Calculate per-60, adjusted percentages, inverted metrics, percentiles by position group, category scores, and overall scores.
Use games played, TOI, shot/chance attempts, and metric-specific denominators.
If a metric is null or inactive, redistribute weight within the active category.
Roster table, top skaters, top goalies, category leaders, risk flags, and sample-size flags.
Cards for overall/category scores, strongest KPIs, development areas, comparison to team/position average, raw stat table.
Goalie overall score, save %, GSAE, chance save profile, workload, puck-playing, reliability.
Compare players or goalies across raw, calculated, and weighted metrics.
Every score should have a tooltip showing source columns, direction, normalization, and weight.
Upload the current Dal skater + goalie exports and confirm scores produce without errors, nulls are handled, and active weights sum correctly.
If include metric is null/unavailable, reweight available metrics within category
